findings.quote: Learning the cello is the most effective way to sharpen an aging mind.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick C. A is an eliminable superlative ('most effective') and D overclaims ('proves'); B (childhood training) is off the study's frame, leaving the balanced C, but B/C scope needs the passage.

findings.quote: Every participant improved dramatically once the sixteen-week course had ended.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick A. B is an eliminable absolute ('every... dramatically') and D is a generalization not about these volunteers; modest A is the test-wise pick, but C (a specific hours figure) can only be ruled out by the passage.

findings.quote: The readers showed no measurable improvement on the attention tasks at all.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick D. A ('no... at all') and B ('more... on every measure') are eliminable absolutes and C breaks the logic of a comparison group; D is left as the moderate pick, but confirming it needs the passage.

findings.quote: The particular skills of cello playing, such as timing and finger control.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick B. Stem says 'both groups', so cello-specific A cannot be responsible and is eliminable by logic; C is an absolute ('fully accounted for every gain'); B (shared routine) vs D (test-retest confound) is the real crux the passage decides.

findings.quote: Dismissive: the writer treats the claim as obvious wishful nonsense.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick A. The two extremes B ('Convinced') and C ('Dismissive') are eliminable as overstated tones, leaving reserved A vs optimistic D; A is the test-wise lean but the writer's exact stance needs the passage.

findings.quote: Margins are narrow, and a café that misjudges its stock can erode its slim margins overnight
findings.note: class 4: register/house-style — the noun "margins" is repeated within one sentence ("Margins are narrow ... its slim margins"), an audible echo a serious-magazine editor would strike; rewrite: "Margins are narrow, and a café that misjudges its stock can wipe out its slender takings overnight"

findings.quote: Keepers were paid only when ships arrived safely, so keeping the lamps lit served their own interests.
findings.note: World-knowledge lean, not a form leak. The pay-on-safe-arrival principal-agent moral makes D the recognizable 'best pick' to anyone who knows incentive economics, but C (fixed toll = steady income), A (council oversight) and B (regional standards) are all substantive, passage-groundable alternatives. Crucially C and D BOTH build on the payment fact, so the pre-redesign structural leak (a lone payment option among non-payment fillers) is gone; from form alone the four options are parallel and A/B cannot be dropped without the passage. Genuine comprehension question -> WORLD_KNOWLEDGE flag, not kill; no structural leak survives the redesign.

findings.quote: Lyktans sken överröstar det svaga horisontljus som fjärilarna orienterar sig efter, så att de cirklar kring lampan i stället.
findings.note: WORLD_KNOWLEDGE, blind_pick A. Moth phototaxis (insects circling artificial light because it swamps the horizon-glow they navigate by) is well-known; B (burns eyes), C (flee) are naive misconceptions and D (scent disappears) is a category error, so a domain-aware solver leans A blind. Distractors are substantive; the exact reason attributed to Vikbrandt still needs the passage.

findings.quote: Konstgjord belysning slår sannolikt ut pollineringen nästan överallt och bör därför avvecklas i så gott som hela landskapet.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick C. Main-idea stem: B is an eliminable policy overreach, A and D are too-narrow details, leaving the balanced C as the test-wise pick - materially better than chance but the A/C/D scope is not decided without the passage.

findings.quote: Att kultursatsningar aldrig går att mäta på ett meningsfullt sätt.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick B. C is an eliminable absolute ('aldrig'), A and D are narrow specifics unlikely to be 'the main objection', leaving the moderate B; better than chance from form, but B vs C turns on the passage.

findings.quote: Förstagångsbesökarnas nyfikenhet på de nya magasinen.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick null. Stem asks about RETURNING visitors, so A (first-timers) is a stem mismatch and D ('saknade helt betydelse') is an eliminable absolute; the real B-vs-C opposition (exhibitions vs cafes) needs the passage.

findings.quote: Enbart kaféerna; utställningarna saknade helt betydelse för besökarna.
findings.note: ARGUABLE: like the key C, D points to the cafés, and the passage says 'tycks kaféerna, inte utställningarna, ha varit det som lockade dem tillbaka' — a reader could stretch 'inte utställningarna' into 'exhibitions had no importance'. Beatable: key C mirrors the hedge with 'snarare än', while D over-reaches with 'Enbart'/'saknade helt betydelse', which the hedged 'tycks' and the narrow scope (what brought them BACK) do not support. Key still clearly best; surfaced for adjudication as the closest call.

findings.quote: Ett herbarium säger i själva verket ingenting om växterna, bara om den som samlat dem.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick A. C is an eliminable absolute ('ingenting... bara') and D is a narrow overclaim; the integrative A vs the divergence-claim B is the real crux the passage decides.

findings.quote: Selmas bok rymmer enbart vetenskap, medan Heddas enbart rymmer känslor.
findings.note: PARTIALLY, blind_pick null. C is an eliminable absolute ('enbart... enbart') and B is a non-parallel pairing; A and D form a clean reversal pair whose direction only the passage settles.
